title: My English Voice: Grade 7 Capstone Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Angry and ready to fight or argue.
choices: belligerent
vindictive
charismatic
rounds.answer: belligerent
rounds.explanation: belligerent: Angry and ready to fight or argue.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of sound or movement.
choices: belligerent
vindictive
rhythm
rounds.answer: rhythm
rounds.explanation: rhythm: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of sound or movement.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A way of explaining something by comparing it to something else that is similar.
choices: vindictive
analogy
belligerent
rounds.answer: analogy
rounds.explanation: analogy: A way of explaining something by comparing it to something else that is similar.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The wish to do evil or harm to others.
rounds.answer: malevolence

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Doing or saying something again and again.
rounds.answer: repetition

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A way of explaining something by comparing it to something else that is similar.
rounds.answer: analogy

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: The belligerent dog barked loudly at anyone who came near.
It can infuriate me when people talk during a movie.
The students will collaborate to create posters about protecting the environment.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: A feeling of sadness or being unhappy for a long time.
Able to be easily hurt or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
Careful about possible dangers or problems.
Very happy and excited about something good that has happened.
rounds.answer: A feeling of sadness or being unhappy for a long time.
rounds.explanation: After her friend moved away, she felt a deep sense of melancholy.

choices: Able to be easily hurt or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
Careful about possible dangers or problems.
Full of energy, excitement, and happiness.
Very hungry or starving.
rounds.answer: Full of energy, excitement, and happiness.
rounds.explanation: The children were exuberant as they played in the park on a sunny day.

choices: A serious effort or attempt to do something.
To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.
To create or start something that is meant to last.
To show how something works or to prove something clearly.
rounds.answer: To accept or admit that something is true, or to recognise that you have received something.
rounds.explanation: I want to acknowledge that you did a great job on your project.

games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.answer: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.

choices: Angry and ready to fight or argue.

Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.
rounds.answer: Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.

Accept an accurate response supported by the selected review text.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: Explain four portfolio choices and support each choice with precise evidence.
It can infuriate me when people talk during a movie.
six audience questions with relevant evidence, source awareness and thoughtful clarification.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Learning to navigate with a compass was a worthwhile endeavour.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Through perseverance, she learned to speak English fluently.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: I want to express my ideas in the school magazine.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'melancholy' mean in this unit?
choices: A feeling of sadness or being unhappy for a long time.
Angry and ready to fight or argue.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
rounds.answer: A feeling of sadness or being unhappy for a long time.
rounds.explanation: melancholy: A feeling of sadness or being unhappy for a long time.

rounds.prompt: What does 'bias' mean in this unit?
choices: Angry and ready to fight or argue.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
An unfair preference or opinion that affects how you think or act.
rounds.answer: An unfair preference or opinion that affects how you think or act.
rounds.explanation: bias: An unfair preference or opinion that affects how you think or act.

rounds.prompt: What does 'vindictive' mean in this unit?
choices: Having a special charm that attracts and inspires people.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
Angry and ready to fight or argue.
rounds.answer: Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
rounds.explanation: vindictive: Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

choices: Verb
Noun
Adjective
rounds.answer: Verb
rounds.explanation: collaborate is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

choices: Verb
Adjective
Noun
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: achievement is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

choices: Verb
Adjective
Noun
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: diligent is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

pairs: Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
Having a special charm that attracts and inspires people.
The repetition of the same sound at the beginning of several words in a sentence or...
A feeling of sadness or being unhappy for a long time.
Able to be easily hurt or harmed, either physically or emotionally.
To show how something works or to prove something clearly.
An unfair preference or opinion that affects how you think or act.
Being fully committed to a task or purpose; giving time and energy to something.
To show or tell what you think or feel.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

choices: Hard-working and very dedicated to tasks.
Having a special charm that attracts and inspires people.
Showing great excitement and interest about something.
Working hard and being careful to do things well.
rounds.answer: Having a special charm that attracts and inspires people.
rounds.explanation: The charismatic leader inspired everyone to work together for a better school.

choices: A strong, regular, repeated pattern of sound or movement.
Doing or saying something again and again.
The marks used in writing to separate sentences and clarify meaning, like full stops, commas, and question marks.
The repetition of the same sound at the beginning of several words in a sentence or phrase.
rounds.answer: The repetition of the same sound at the beginning of several words in a sentence or phrase.
rounds.explanation: "Isaac Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers" is a famous example of alliteration.

choices: A word that has the same ending sound as another word.
Doing or saying something again and again.
The marks used in writing to separate sentences and clarify meaning, like full stops, commas, and question marks.
Using words in a way that is not literal, often to create a special meaning or effect.
rounds.answer: A word that has the same ending sound as another word.
rounds.explanation: In the poem, "cat" and "hat" are examples of words that rhyme.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

choices: A strong feeling of dislike or hatred towards someone.
Angry and ready to fight or argue.
To make someone very angry.
Wanting to hurt someone because they hurt you.
rounds.answer: Angry and ready to fight or argue.
rounds.explanation: The belligerent dog barked loudly at anyone who came near.

choices: A strong feeling of dislike or hatred towards someone.
Someone who is very interested in themselves and thinks they are better than others.
The wish to do evil or harm to others.
To make someone very angry.
rounds.answer: The wish to do evil or harm to others.
rounds.explanation: The villain in the story showed malevolence by trying to hurt the hero.

choices: Having a strong desire to achieve something and not giving up.
Kind and generous; wanting to help others.
Showing great excitement and interest about something.
Working hard and being careful to do things well.
rounds.answer: Kind and generous; wanting to help others.
rounds.explanation: The benevolent woman donated food to the homeless shelter.
